prompt: Implement a production-quality React 18 + TypeScript autocomplete component. Export it as the default function with props { searchUsers }, where searchUsers(query: string, signal: AbortSignal): Promise<Array<{id:string,name:string}>>. Keep the query input controlled by React state. Debounce nonempty queries by exactly 250 ms, cancel pending and in-flight obsolete work with AbortController, and ensure stale responses never replace newer results even if a search implementation ignores abort. Support ArrowDown/ArrowUp navigation with wrapping, Enter selection, Escape dismissal, and mouse selection. Selecting a user puts the name in the input and closes the popup without starting another search. Provide correct combobox/listbox/option ARIA relationships and expose loading, empty, and error feedback accessibly. Empty input performs no search. Use React only; no external component or debounce packages. Return one fenced tsx implementation with no tests or main routine, followed by a concise explanation of races, cleanup, accessibility, and deterministic timer/interaction tests. Keep the answer under 1400 words.

Implement an idempotent POST /orders endpoint in TypeScript using Express and PostgreSQL. Return exactly two files in manifest order. Invalid key or body returns 400. Hash the exact raw bytes with SHA-256. For a successful first request return 201 and {id:string,sku:string,quantity:number}. Concurrent requests with the same user, key, and exact bytes must create one order and return that same 201 payload; the same key with different bytes returns 409 {error:"idempotency_key_reused"}. Enforce a primary or unique key on (user_id, idempotency_key), valid processing/completed state transitions, and an order reference for completed records. Do not create a server listener or tests. Follow the files with concise prose explaining the transaction race protocol and failure handling. Do not include other fenced blocks. Keep the answer under 1700 words.

Implement a bounded single-producer/single-consumer byte ring buffer in portable C11. The producer runs in an interrupt handler and the consumer runs in the main loop. Storage is caller-owned; capacity must be a nonzero power of two; no dynamic allocation is allowed; and push/pop must be non-blocking. A full queue must reject the new byte by returning false without changing existing data; an empty pop returns false. Use C11 atomics with correct memory ordering, avoid undefined behavior, preserve the full declared capacity, and handle unsigned counter wraparound. The selected atomic index type must be lock-free. Return one fenced C implementation that includes ring_buffer.h and does not define main, followed by a concise explanation describing deterministic test cases. Keep the answer under 1300 words.

Implement the freestanding C11 API declared by spi_dma_driver.h for a fictional SPI0 plus DMA0 on a single-core little-endian Cortex-M3 (ARMv7-M, AAPCS/EABI). Include spi_dma_driver.h. A valid init resets all driver state and writes exactly SPI control=0, TX channel control=0, RX channel control=0, DMA status_clear=DMA0_STATUS_ALL, SPI clock_divider=SPI_DMA_CLOCK_DIVIDER, chip_select=deasserted, then SPI control=idle; it does not mask interrupts. An uninitialized driver, NULL TX/RX pointer, zero length, or length above 32 makes start return false without an interrupt or MMIO call. After those basic arguments are valid, start save-disables exactly once and restores the exact state even if it rejects an already busy driver or an unconsumed terminal result. A successful start clears all stale DMA status, asserts chip select, programs and enables RX DMA before TX DMA with the stated direction/IRQ flags and byte count, then writes SPI control=idle|TX_DMA_ENABLE|RX_DMA_ENABLE. It records the active buffers and has no polling, copying, allocation, cache maintenance, or mutable global state. spi_dma_irq runs in the non-nested DMA interrupt and must not save or restore interrupts. When busy, it takes one DMA status snapshot. If any asserted status is observed, it write-one-to-clears exactly those bits. Any TX_ERROR or RX_ERROR wins over simultaneous completions, is accumulated, disables SPI DMA requests and both channels, deasserts chip select, clears active-buffer state, and records SPI_DMA_RESULT_ERROR. Otherwise record each completion independently and perform that same teardown with SPI_DMA_RESULT_COMPLETE only after both channels have completed, even if completion IRQs arrive separately. spi_dma_is_busy and a valid spi_dma_take_result each save-disable once and restore the exact previous state. take_result writes zero through error_flags and returns NONE when no terminal result is pending; otherwise it returns the stored terminal result, writes the stored error flags, and clears the pending result and error flags. A new transfer is permitted only after a pending result is taken. Invalid busy/take calls have no guard or MMIO side effects. Implement a generic TypeScript singleflight cache as one self-contained module. Successful values remain fresh while now() is strictly less than their expiry; ttlMs 0 disables reuse. At most one current loader runs per key, including during synchronous loader execution. A caller may reject its own wait with signal.reason without cancelling the shared load needed by others, and an already-aborted caller must not invoke or join a loader. Loader rejections and synchronous throws are returned as promise rejections and are not cached. invalidate(key) removes a cached value, detaches the current in-flight load, and prevents that older load from repopulating or overwriting newer data; clear() provides the same guarantee for every key. Expiry must be lazy with no per-entry timers. Use no external packages. Return one fenced TypeScript implementation with no tests or main routine, followed by a concise explanation of race handling, complexity, memory bounds, and deterministic fake-clock tests. Keep the answer under 1600 words.

Implement the declared two-file TypeScript/SQL webhook fixture.
